mentions.note: Still has premium multiple from growth, but unclear if capable of beating numbers anymore; package food business is 'new coal' in this market.
Merger talks with Unilever food business (Hellmann's, Coleman's, Knorr); combined company could unlock cost synergies via new distribution; love the combo if announced.
Unilever food deal (Hellmann's, Colman's, Knorr) accretive and strategic but ignored in growth market; 16x earnings, 3.81% yield, trades at discount to intrinsic value.

Transformational Unilever Foods deal (Hellmann's, Knorr, Colman's, Maille) adds scale, global reach; down 42% over 5 years, 29% in March (worst month 46 yrs)—worries priced in; flavor positioning strong in GLP-1 era.
CEO Foley given free pass on Unilever Hellmann's acquisition cost; deal price questioned by Street; should have challenged synergy assumptions vs. packaged food headwinds.
Hit new lows; Unilever deal benefit not being recognized; spice/flavor market out of favor.
Stock crushed; Wall Street thinks Unilever food business acquisition is colossal overpay. Waiting to hear management's justification at earnings.
